goal.text: Align create-claw-skill with the new boundary where SKILL.md carries routing and non-template supplements, template-owned structured data stays in the plan/template, and review the modified local superpowers skills against that same standard.

requirements.summary: Update claw-kit's create-claw-skill implementation and guidance so it no longer treats CLAW-KNOWLEDGE.md as a default extra layer. The generated claw skill should prefer keeping route rules and non-template supplement material in SKILL.md, keep structured workflow and execution data in TEMPLATE.json, and use skill-local references only when needed. Then review the locally modified installed superpowers skills against this new standard and fix any mismatches that are clearly wrong.
acceptanceCriteria: The create-claw-skill stub no longer generates CLAW-KNOWLEDGE.md by default.
The create-claw-skill template and docs describe the new boundary: SKILL.md for routing and non-template supplements; template for structured rules, references, deliverables, and workflow control.
Any generated references guidance points to skill-local references rather than a mandatory CLAW-KNOWLEDGE.md layer.
A review covers the modified local installed superpowers skills and reports findings against the new boundary.
Any obvious boundary violations in the modified local installed superpowers skills are corrected or explicitly called out.

rules: Do not reintroduce CLAW-KNOWLEDGE.md as a default generated artifact.
Prefer moving structured deliverables, references, rules, and workflow control into TEMPLATE.json or plan-owned surfaces.
Allow SKILL.md to repeat emphasized constraints when that improves routing clarity or prevents misuse.
Review the modified local installed superpowers skills against the new boundary rather than against the older compiled-knowledge design.

references.why: Stub generator currently emits CLAW-KNOWLEDGE.md and old surface guidance.
Template authoring guide needs boundary language aligned.
Design lessons currently describe compiled knowledge references.
Installed local superpowers skills to review against the new standard.
